meta.query: (all:"document parsing" OR all:"document understanding" OR all:"optical character recognition" OR all:OCR OR all:"layout analysis" OR all:"document layout analysis" OR all:"text recognition" OR all:"table recognition" OR all:"form understanding" OR all:"document intelligence" OR all:"page understanding" OR all:"scene text recognition" OR all:"handwritten text recognition" OR all:"information extraction") AND (cat:cs.CV OR cat:cs.AI OR cat:cs.CL OR cat:eess.IV)

synthesis.executive_summary: 今日研究聚焦于提升OCR和文档解析在实际场景中的鲁棒性、可审计性与效率。核心趋势包括：视觉语言模型正替代传统OCR+LLM流水线实现端到端文档理解，强调可验证的证据定位；多语言、多布局的标准化基准推动系统评估；通过显式视觉token监督或人机协同标注等创新方法提升细粒度理解与标注精度。这些成果为医疗、历史及多语言文档处理提供了更可靠、可扩展的解决方案。
synthesis.trend_summary: 多模态大模型端到端用于文档理解并强调可解释性与审计性；构建大规模多语言真实文档基准以系统评估能力；探索直接在视觉token上施加监督信号以增强细粒度理解；利用跨模型共识的人机协同策略降低标注成本并提升精度。
common_innovations: 视觉语言模型取代传统OCR+LLM流水线，实现端到端文档理解并保留证据定位能力。
构建多语言、多布局的真实文档评估基准，推动标准化和可复现的ATR测评。
对视觉token施加显式逐token监督，无需额外标注或架构修改即可提升细粒度理解。
提出两层跨模型共识的人机协同标注框架，大幅减少人工审核量并保持高精度。
future_directions: 探索高效微调策略（如LoRA与DV-SFT结合）以减少医疗等场景对大规模标注数据的依赖。
将RAPTOR+框架扩展至更多癌症类型及非结构化体检报告，并设计跨机构域适应方法。
在METATR基准中引入文档退化类型（墨迹、污损、折痕等）以评估模型鲁棒性。
将DV-SFT的视觉token监督推广到OCR之外的通用视觉理解任务（如图表、票据）。
研究双三角形标注框架中模型选择策略，最大化错误独立性以提升共识效率。
开发自适应共识阈值机制，动态平衡自动采纳率与人工审核成本。
结合结构化信息抽取任务设计面向文档的新评估指标（如提取准确率、字段完整性）。
engineering_takeaways: RAPTOR+框架可直接用于开发可审计的医疗文档处理系统，减少人工审查80%以上。
METATR基准可作为选型依据，优先选择在目标语言和布局上表现稳定的VLM或序列模型。
DV-SFT方法可视为标准SFT的免费升级，在OCR类视觉理解任务中优先使用。
双三角形标注框架适合历史文档等标注资源稀缺场景，建议先作POC验证跨模型共识效果。

watchlist.title: RAPTOR+: A Visually Grounded Vision-Language Framework to Improve Clinical Trust and Auditability in Automated Cancer Referral Processing
watchlist.watch_reason: 医疗领域对可信提取需求高，RAPTOR+的端到端证据定位方案有潜力成为行业标杆，需关注后续跨癌种和跨机构泛化实验。

watchlist.title: METATR: A Multilingual, Evolving Benchmark for Automatic Text Recognition
watchlist.watch_reason: 多语言基准是系统评估和选型关键，后续版本扩展语言和退化类型后将更具参考价值，建议跟进并贡献方言或罕见脚本数据。

watchlist.title: DV-SFT: Direct Vision Supervision for Fine-Grained Visual Understanding
watchlist.watch_reason: 视觉token监督信号方法新颖且兼容现有训练流程，若扩展到通用视觉理解，可能成为MLLM微调标配，建议复现验证其效果。

watchlist.title: Double Triangle Annotation: A Scalable Human-in-the-Loop Framework for High-Precision Historical Document Annotation
watchlist.watch_reason: 两层共识框架在历史文档上表现优异，后续如果推广到更多文档类型和模型组合，将显著影响标注行业成本结构。

papers.title: RAPTOR+: A Visually Grounded Vision-Language Framework to Improve Clinical Trust and Auditability in Automated Cancer Referral Processing
papers.summary: Urgent suspected colorectal cancer (CRC) referrals create operational bottlenecks because semi-structured clinical documents often require manual review and transcription. The original RAPTOR system used Large Language Models for structured extraction but relied on a separate OCR stage, making it vulnerable to handwriting, layout variation, and loss of visual evidence linkage. We present RAPTOR+, a multimodal extension that uses Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for end-to-end referral understanding. We evaluate fine-tuned VLMs, commercial and open-source zero-shot VLMs, and the original OCR-based pipeline on 223 clinically curated CRC urgent referral forms. We also introduce a grounding-aware evaluation framework that measures both extraction accuracy and evidence localisation. Results show a clear grounding gap in zero-shot models. Gemini 2.5 Flash achieved 92.6% Reading Accuracy but only 1.2% Strict Safety. In contrast, fine-tuned Qwen3-VL-8B achieved 96.1% Reading Accuracy and 60.6% Strict Safety, substantially improving verifiable evidence grounding. These findings show that task-specific fine-tuning is essential for reliable, auditable clinical document understanding. RAPTOR+ enables extracted referral decisions to be linked to visual evidence, supporting safer and more efficient cancer referral triage.
papers.zh_summary: 针对疑似结直肠癌转诊中半结构化文档人工审核负担重、原始RAPTOR系统依赖OCR易受手写和布局影响的问题，提出RAPTOR+多模态扩展，使用视觉语言模型进行端到端转诊理解。在223份临床转诊表单上，细调Qwen3-VL-8B实现96.1%读取准确率和60.6%严格安全性，显著优于零样本模型。引入可验证的证据定位评估框架。
papers.novelty: 将视觉语言模型端到端应用于临床转诊文档理解，并设计考虑证据定位的评估方法。
innovation_points: 提出多模态视觉语言模型RAPTOR+，替代OCR+LLM流水线进行端到端临床文档理解。
设计基于证据定位的评估框架，同时衡量提取准确性和证据定位能力。
在真实临床数据集上对比细调与零样本VLM性能，证明任务特定微调的必要性。
papers.limitations: 仅针对结直肠癌转诊场景，泛化到其他癌症类型或医疗文档有待验证；微调需要标注数据，实际部署成本较高。
improvement_directions: 探索更高效的微调策略，减少对大规模标注数据的依赖。
将框架扩展到更多类型的医疗文档和癌症筛查流程。
研究跨机构部署时的域适应问题。
papers.engineering_value: 为医疗文档自动化处理提供可靠、可审计的解决方案，可减少人工审查工作量，提高转诊效率。
papers.why_relevant: 直接面向OCR鲁棒性挑战，通过多模态方法提升文档理解的可信度，对工业级OCR系统有启发。

papers.title: METATR: A Multilingual, Evolving Benchmark for Automatic Text Recognition
papers.summary: Benchmarks that reflect the diversity and complexity of real-world documents are essential for accurately evaluating Automatic Text Recognition (ATR) systems, especially Vision-Large Language Models (vLLMs). Although recent models demonstrate impressive performance, they are often evaluated on datasets containing modern, printed texts mostly written in English, which limits their relevance to many practical applications. Therefore, selecting a model for a specific use case requires evaluating it on data that matches the target documents. This highlights the importance of representative benchmarks for real-world applications. In this paper, we introduce METATR (v1.0), a multilingual, evolving benchmark designed to evaluate ATR models across a wide range of documents, facilitating meaningful model comparison and selection. The benchmark was designed to maximize diversity by including documents from various public collections. These documents cover 29 languages and include texts with multiple scripts and layouts. Beyond the dataset itself, METATR defines a standardized prompting and normalization methodology and establishes a dynamic evaluation framework. This approach is intended to produce reproducible results while remaining extensible over time. We evaluated a wide range of state-of-the-art systems, including open-source models and closed-source models. Results are reported across various dimensions, including performance at the dataset and language levels, robustness to handwritten documents, and computational efficiency. Our findings show that, although proprietary models achieve the most consistent performance, substantial variability persists across scripts and layouts. Overall, METATR provides a multidimensional, practitioner-oriented framework for assessing multilingual ATR in real-world conditions and tracking progress as the field evolves.
papers.zh_summary: 提出METATR多语言、渐进式自动文本识别基准，包含29种语言、多种手写和印刷文档。设计标准化提示和归一化方法，评估多种开源和闭源系统，揭示在多样化真实文档上模型性能的差异。
papers.novelty: 构建覆盖多语言、多脚本、多布局的真实文档基准，并定义可复现的评估协议。
innovation_points: 构建包含29种语言、多种书写风格和布局的评估数据集。
设计标准化的提示词和归一化方法，确保评估结果可复现。
提出动态评估框架，支持未来扩展新模型和数据集。
papers.limitations: 当前版本v1.0，覆盖文档类型和语言有限；未深入评估模型对噪声和退化的鲁棒性。
improvement_directions: 扩展更多低资源语言和罕见脚本的样本。
引入更丰富的文档退化类型（如墨迹、污损）。
发展基于任务的评估指标，如信息提取准确率。
papers.engineering_value: 为ATR系统选型提供真实场景参考，促进模型在跨国、多语言文档处理中的应用。
papers.why_relevant: 直接服务于OCR领域，解决现有基准缺乏多样性的问题，对任务模型评估和选择有实际指导意义。

papers.title: DV-SFT: Direct Vision Supervision for Fine-Grained Visual Understanding
papers.summary: Multimodal large language models are typically trained end-to-end to predict ground-truth answers, yet supervision signals are applied exclusively to text tokens. Visual tokens, the core carriers of visual information, are optimized only implicitly as part of the context, leading to coarse-grained visual understanding. Prior works attempt to supervise visual inputs but inevitably rely on auxiliary components such as additional decoders or forward passes, because visual tokens lack readily interpretable labels. This limits their practical applicability. In this work, we propose \textbf{D}irect \textbf{V}ision \textbf{S}upervised \textbf{F}ine-\textbf{T}uning (DV-SFT), which constructs explicit, token-level supervision for visual tokens and trains them through the same next-token prediction objective used for text. Specifically, we exploit the direct vision--text correspondence in OCR-related scenarios and automatically label each visual token with the word in its corresponding image patch. DV-SFT treats the MLLM as a black box, requiring no architectural modifications or additional forward passes. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of direct vision supervision. DV-SFT consistently outperforms standard SFT across three in-domain and four out-of-domain benchmarks. Further analyses show that vision supervision effectively enhances fine-grained visual understanding and achieves higher multimodal alignment efficiency.
papers.zh_summary: 提出直接视觉监督微调方法，通过为视觉token构造显式token级监督信号，与文本token采用相同下一个词预测目标训练。利用OCR场景中视觉-文本对应关系自动标注视觉token，无需架构修改或额外前向传播。实验表明该方法在三个域内和四个域外基准上均优于标准SFT。
papers.novelty: 首次在MLLM中对视觉token施加显式逐token监督，且不依赖额外组件。
innovation_points: 提出视觉token的显式逐token监督信号构造方法。
利用OCR场景的视觉-文本对应关系自动标注，实现零额外标注成本。
无需修改模型架构，兼容现有训练流程。
papers.limitations: 依赖OCR场景的视觉-文本对应关系，通用场景下难以直接应用；监督信号粒度与OCR标注质量相关。
improvement_directions: 探索将视觉监督扩展到OCR以外的通用视觉理解任务。
研究自动标注的质量控制机制，减少噪声。
结合自监督预训练，增强视觉token语义表达。
papers.engineering_value: 提供一种提升MLLM细粒度视觉理解能力的轻量训练方案，可无缝集成到现有微调流程。
papers.why_relevant: 直接针对OCR场景中的视觉理解瓶颈，提出的方法可提升OCR相关任务性能。

papers.title: Double Triangle Annotation: A Scalable Human-in-the-Loop Framework for High-Precision Historical Document Annotation
papers.summary: Evaluating structured-information extraction from historical documents at scale requires high-precision ground-truth annotations, yet traditional manual labeling is expensive and fully automated pipelines built on large language models are prone to hallucination. We propose Double Triangle Annotation, a two-layer human-in-the-loop framework that leverages cross-model consensus to automate the majority of annotation work while ensuring high-precision outputs. In the first layer, two architecturally independent Multimodal Large Language Models annotate each document in parallel; when they agree, the label is auto-accepted, and disagreements are routed to a human jury. A second layer cross-checks two such systems against each other, escalating residual conflicts to a domain expert. The framework rests on a single assumption -- error independence between models -- requires no distributional priors or task-specific calibration, and becomes more autonomous as model capability improves. On the Guides Rosenwald, a corpus of French medical directories spanning 1887-1906, the framework achieves a final Word Error Rate of 0.003. Applied at scale, model consensus auto-accepts over 85% of 13,595 fields. We release the resulting benchmark -- the first structured-extraction ground truth for the Rosenwald Guides -- to support future work on historical document processing.
papers.zh_summary: 提出双三角形标注框架，利用跨模型共识实现高精度历史文档标注。两层人机协同：第一层两独立MLLM并行标注，一致则自动采纳，分歧交人工仲裁；第二层交叉检查两套系统，残余冲突由专家处理。在法国医学目录数据集上实现0.003词错误率，85%字段自动采纳。
papers.novelty: 提出基于模型共识的两层人机协同标注框架，无需分布先验或任务特定校准。
innovation_points: 设计两层人机协同标注框架，利用跨模型共识减少人工投入。
假设模型间错误独立性，无需任务特定校准。
在历史文档上实现高精度标注，并释放首个结构化提取基准。
papers.limitations: 依赖多模型同时运行，计算成本较高；错误独立性假设在场景相似模型上可能不成立。
improvement_directions: 研究模型选择策略，以最大化错误独立性。
探索模型共识阈值自适应调整方法。
应用到更广泛的历史文档类型和语言。
papers.engineering_value: 为大规模历史文档标注提供可扩展的解决方案，平衡成本与精度，适用于数字人文。
papers.why_relevant: 解决历史文档OCR标注困难的问题，提供高精度数据生产方法，推动领域发展。
